key_insight: VASP plugins use a Python-facing architecture to add or prototype features in a production DFT code without sacrificing performance.
engineering_relevance: This makes it easier to extend legacy HPC electronic-structure software with modern scientific Python tools while avoiding costly data copying.
method: C++ intermediate layer exposes VASP data to Python via pybind11
Shared-memory NumPy buffers provide zero-duplication data access
Two plugin modes: post-SCF updates and in-SCF operations
evidence_signal: The abstract reports three demonstration applications: structure relaxation with SciPy, an implicit solvent model, and DFT-D4 dispersion corrections.
watch_item: Because plugins interact with a complex legacy codebase and shared-memory data flow, correctness and synchronization across SCF iterations must be handled carefully.
paper_title: VASP Plugins: Linking the Vienna ab-initio Simulation Package with Python
